 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.5.1","title":"Docs: README quick install + verification snippet","description":"# Goal
Update `README.md` to include the canonical **operator install** commands and verification steps.

---

# Requirements
- Show the “happy path” one-liner:
  - `curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash -s -- --easy-mode --verify`
- Show verification commands:
  - `brenner doctor`
  - `ntm deps -v`
  - `cass health`
  - `cm onboard status`

Also include a pinned-tag install option once releases exist.

---

# Acceptance criteria
- README contains a dedicated Install section with copy/paste commands.
- README instructions match the actual installer flags.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.5.2","title":"Docs: troubleshooting + upgrade runbook (PATH, tmux, proxies, Windows)","description":"# Goal
Write a self-contained troubleshooting runbook for bootstrap installs.

---

# Topics to cover
- PATH not updated (zsh/bash/fish)
- macOS Gatekeeper prompts (if we ship unsigned binaries)
- missing `tmux` (ntm dependency) + how to install
- corporate proxy / SSL issues with `curl` + GitHub
- Windows quirks:
  - where `install.ps1` installs
  - how PATH update works
  - why `ntm` is skipped

# Upgrade story
- “upgrade via re-running installer”
- `brenner upgrade` (once implemented)
- toolchain manifest pinning and how to bump versions intentionally

---

# Acceptance criteria
- A new operator can recover from common failures without reading source.
- Runbook aligns with actual installer flags and `brenner doctor` output.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.6","title":"Repo hygiene: ignore generated dirs (node_modules, coverage, playwright)","description":"# Goal
Prevent accidental commits and tooling noise from generated directories.

# Background
- This repo currently has a minimal `.gitignore` and generated directories commonly appear in working trees (`node_modules`, Playwright reports, coverage, Next build output, etc.).
- This increases risk of accidentally committing artifacts and also makes tools like `ubs` noisy when scanning the repo.

# Current risk
`git ls-files` shows at least one tracked file under `node_modules/` (likely a generated Vitest cache file).

Per AGENTS.md, removing it from git requires a destructive git operation; I need explicit user-provided approval before I can run or propose the exact command.

# Scope
- Expand `.gitignore` with common generated directories and files:
  - `node_modules/`
  - `apps/web/node_modules/`
  - `apps/web/.next/`
  - `apps/web/playwright-report/`
  - `apps/web/test-results/`
  - `apps/web/coverage/`
  - `dist/`
  - `*.tsbuildinfo`

# Acceptance criteria
- `.gitignore` updates prevent new generated artifacts from being staged by default.
- Follow-up is documented for cleaning the already-tracked `node_modules/` artifact (requires explicit approval).

NOTE: 1 tracked file remains under node_modules/ and will require explicit user-approved untracking.","labels":["ci","git","repo-hygiene"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.6","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T08:13:59.284129176-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12","title":"EPIC: Science Mode v0 (Deterministic roles + experiment loop)","description":"# Goal
Make BrennerBot **runnable as a real multi-agent research lab** on a subject we choose *today* — not just “protocol theater”.

“Science Mode v0” means we can repeatedly do:
**propose → run a discriminative test → capture results → update artifact**

…while preserving Brenner-style hygiene:
- explicit hypotheses (including a real third alternative)
- discriminative tests + potency checks
- provenance + citations (no vibes)
- anomaly quarantine instead of rationalization

---

# Key decision (v0): `project_key` == the research workspace
We avoid cross-repo “target binding” complexity in v0.

**Rule:** Start the session *in the repo/folder you’re actually studying* (codebase, dataset workspace, etc.).

Why:
- Agent Mail is already keyed by `project_key` (absolute directory).
- File reservations naturally scope to the workspace.
- Artifacts/results/evidence live next to what they’re about.
- This preserves the join-key contract without inventing new binding types.

Corollary:
- `--project-key "$PWD"` is the binding.
- We should only add explicit `--target-repo` style binding if we later prove we need cross-project sessions.

---

# What’s missing today
We have kickoff → deltas → compile. What’s still missing for real research:

1) **Deterministic role assignment** for real Agent Mail identities (adjective+noun names like `BlueLake`).
2) **External evidence plumbing** so sessions can cite papers/datasets/prior results with stable IDs.
3) **Experiment capture plumbing** so a test run becomes a durable, structured record (not terminal scrollback).
4) **A minimal closed-loop operator workflow** that connects (1)-(3) into one repeatable loop.

---

# Scope (v0)
## In scope
- Explicit agent roster mapping (CLI + web lab UI)
- Experiment execution wrapper:
  - operator-provided command
  - capture stdout/stderr/exit code/duration
  - `--timeout` support
  - write a structured `ExperimentResult` JSON file under a session-scoped folder
- Deterministic encoder that turns an `ExperimentResult` into **non-interpretive** DELTAs
  - e.g. “T1 ran at time X, exit code Y, results file Z”
  - *interpretation* remains a separate, guided step
- External evidence import + citation support (papers/datasets/prior sessions)
- Runbook for “science mode” happy path

## Explicit non-goals (v0)
- Hard sandboxing / auto-running untrusted code (operators stay in the loop)
- Web-hosted execution on production (lab mode only)
- Full bibliographic manager or PDF ingestion pipeline

---

# Definition of done
- Operator can start a role-separated session in a chosen workspace and compile a canonical artifact.
- Operator can run a test command, capture a structured result JSON, post a DELTA referencing it, and compile an updated artifact.
- Artifacts can cite external evidence with stable IDs and preserve provenance.
- The workflow is documented well enough that a future operator can run it without inventing new glue.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.1","title":"Feature: Agent roster + deterministic role assignment (CLI + web)","description":"# Goal
Make **role-separated multi-agent sessions** work with real Agent Mail identities (adjective+noun names) *deterministically*.

Today, role assignment is heuristic (string matching on recipient name). That fails for real identities like `BlueLake` / `PurpleMountain`.

This feature introduces an explicit roster mapping so that:
- the operator selects or configures which agent is "GPT/Hypotheses", "Opus/Tests", "Gemini/Critique"
- kickoffs sent via CLI/web contain the correct role block for each recipient
- the roster becomes part of session metadata (auditable + reproducible)

---

# Why this matters
The whole Brenner Loop relies on **division of labor**:
- one agent generates hypotheses
- one designs discriminative tests + potency checks
- one attacks framing + scale checks

If everyone receives the same prompt or an incorrect role prompt, we lose the multi-model advantage and converge to consensus mush.

---

# Requirements
- Roster config supports:
  - mapping `agent_mail_name -> role`
  - optional metadata: program (`codex-cli`, `claude-code`, `gemini-cli`), model string, notes
  - presets ("3-agent default")
- CLI supports:
  - `session start` with explicit per-recipient role mapping (flags or config)
  - validates roster completeness (must cover all recipients)
  - emits the roster mapping into kickoff message body (for audit)
- Web supports:
  - selecting recipients from Agent Mail agent directory
  - assigning roles in UI (dropdown per recipient)
  - using the same underlying kickoff composition logic as the CLI

---

# UX considerations
- Keep the happy path fast:
  - a "Default 3-agent roster" button that assigns roles automatically
  - a way to save/load roster presets (config)
- Fail loudly and early:
  - if roster mapping is missing for any recipient, block the send with a clear message

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Role assignment never depends on substring heuristics in production paths
- [ ] CLI can start a role-separated session using explicit roster mapping
- [ ] Web can start a role-separated session using explicit roster mapping
- [ ] Kickoff messages include an explicit "Role" section and roster provenance
- [ ] Unit tests cover role mapping edge cases
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:57:57.792553571-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T00:16:40.785905698-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T00:16:40.785905698-05:00","close_reason":"All 6 subtasks completed: schema defined (5so.12.1.1), kickoff accepts roles (5so.12.1.2), CLI --role-map implemented (5so.12.1.3), web UI role assignment (5so.12.1.4), doctor checks (5so.12.1.5), docs updated (5so.12.1.6). Role assignment is now deterministic via explicit roster mapping.","labels":["agents","orchestration","roster","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:57:57.813863046-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.1.1","title":"Task: Define agent roster schema + config contract","description":"# Goal
Define a canonical, versioned representation of an **agent roster** and how it is loaded (flags/env/config/defaults).

---

# Design constraints
- Must work for both:
  - `brenner` CLI
  - `apps/web` lab mode
- Must reference **Agent Mail identity names** (e.g., `BlueLake`), not tool names.
- Must be auditable: roster should be serializable into kickoff message bodies and/or session metadata.

---

# Proposed schema (draft)
```ts
type AgentRole = 'hypothesis_generator' | 'test_designer' | 'adversarial_critic';

type AgentRosterEntry = {
  agentName: string;          // Agent Mail identity name
  role: AgentRole;
  program?: 'codex-cli' | 'claude-code' | 'gemini-cli' | string;
  model?: string;             // human-readable
  notes?: string;
};

type AgentRosterPreset = {
  name: string;               // e.g. 'default-3-agent'
  entries: AgentRosterEntry[];
};

type BrennerConfig = {
  // ...existing...
  roster?: {
    presets?: AgentRosterPreset[];
    defaultPreset?: string;
  };
};
```

---

# Loading / precedence
- CLI flags override config.
- Web uses env defaults + server-side config, but still allows per-session overrides.

---

# Edge cases to decide now
- If multiple recipients share a role: allowed? (Probably yes for "more brains" mode, but requires role prompt semantics.)
- If a recipient role is omitted: error (preferred) vs fallback to DEFAULT_ROLE.
- How to represent "unified" mode alongside roster mode.

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Schema written down as a repo contract (spec + types)
- [ ] Precedence rules documented
- [ ] Clear stance on edge cases (duplicate roles, missing mapping)

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.1.2","title":"Task: Session kickoff accepts explicit per-recipient roles","description":"# Goal
Update kickoff composition so role assignment is **explicit** and not inferred from recipient name.

---

# Current problem
`getAgentRole(agentName: string)` uses substring heuristics (`includes('codex')`, etc). Real Agent Mail identities are adjective+noun and won't match.

---

# Proposed API change (example)
Option A (minimal): extend KickoffConfig
```ts
export interface KickoffConfig {
  // existing fields...
  recipients: string[];
  recipientRoles?: Record<string, AgentRole>; // maps recipient -> role
}
```
Option B (cleaner): accept structured recipients
```ts
export interface KickoffRecipient {
  name: string;
  role: AgentRole;
}
export interface KickoffConfig {
  recipients: KickoffRecipient[];
}
```

---

# Considerations
- Must remain deterministic.
- Must keep a reasonable fallback for legacy callers (if any).
- Kickoff body should include the role assignment explicitly so recipients don't rely on memory.

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Role mapping can be provided explicitly for all recipients
- [ ] No substring heuristics in the "explicit roster" path
- [ ] Unit tests: BlueLake-style names still get correct roles
